name: Castilian-Style Garlic Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 cups day-old country bread, crusts removed, cut into half-inch slices then torn roughly into crouton-size pieces
4 cloves garlic, lightly crushed and peeled
4 cups beef broth or beef consomme
6 tablespoons Spanish mountain cured ham. prosciutto, or capicollo, chopped - diced into 1/4 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a skillet, heat 5 teaspoons of olive oil and the minced garlic over low heat until the garlic begins to color. Add the bread pieces and sauté slowly until they are crisp and golden, about 15 minutes. Sprinkle in 1 teaspoon of paprika and stir to coat the bread. Set aside.
2. To make the garlic broth, heat the remaining tablespoon of olive oil in a soup pot and lightly sauté the crushed garlic cloves. Stir in the remaining paprika, water, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes until about 4 cups are reached (these steps can be completed ahead of time).
3. Heat the beef broth in a separate saucepan until boiling. Arrange ovenproof soup bowls on a baking tray and add 1 tablespoon of beef broth to each bowl. If using eggs, place one into each bowl, scatter the ham pieces over them, and pour additional beef broth so the bowls are half full. Place the tray with the bowls in a preheated oven at 450°F until the eggs are set for about 4 minutes.
4. Pour the reserved garlic broth into the bowls, hot from the pot, and add the bread pieces on top. Serve immediately.
